    Final Project Data Overview

    Please visit the data directory on Github: Click here, and read the introduction below:

    MovieMap — Data

    This directory holds every dataset used by the MovieMap scrollytelling site and its embedded Tableau dashboards. Each CSV here was either downloaded directly from a public open-data portal or scraped from a public web source and cleaned into a wide- or long-format table that Tableau can consume.

    data/
├── sf_film/        San Francisco filming locations + Film SF impact reports
├── hengdian/       Hengdian World Studios — attractions, productions, photos, shapefile
└── imdb/           IMDb Top 250 metadata + a 91-movie location corpus

    The site uses each dataset as follows:

    Site section Data used
    SF filming-locations map (Section 01) sf_film/Film_Locations_in_San_Francisco_20260510.csv
    SF film-economy dashboard (Section 01) the seven other CSVs in sf_film/
    Hengdian theme-park map (Section 01) hengdian/hengdian_attractions.csv + hengdian/shapefiles/
    Hengdian productions (tooltip, Section 01) hengdian/hengdian_productions.csv
    IMDb genre / box-office dashboard (Section 02) imdb/imdb_top250_clean.csv
    3D Globe Explorer (Section 03) imdb/movie_location_corpus/

    sf_film/ — San Francisco

    Film_Locations_in_San_Francisco_20260510.csv (2,214 rows)

    Every permitted filming location in the City of San Francisco, with the production’s title, year, fun-fact note, and a POINT(lng lat) geocode.

    • Source: DataSF — Film Locations in San Francisco https://data.sfgov.org/Culture-and-Recreation/Film-Locations-in-San-Francisco/yitu-d5am
    • Snapshot date: 2026-05-10 (filename suffix)
    • Grain: one (title × location) pair

    Film SF annual impact reports (six CSVs)

    Scraped from the four annual impact reports published by Film SF, the San Francisco Office of Economic and Workforce Development’s film commission, and broken out into one table per metric family. fiscal_year is the calendar year in which the fiscal year ends (so 2025 = FY24-25 = July 2024 – June 2025).

    File Grain Columns of note
    filming_statistics.csv (4 rows) one fiscal year direct economic output, permit fees, shoot days, productions
    funding.csv (4 rows) one fiscal year permit fees, general fund, Grants for the Arts, total budget
    productions_by_type.csv (44 rows) fiscal year × production type productions, shoot days per type (WEB, FEATURE, TV SERIES, etc.)
    jurisdictions.csv (4 rows) one fiscal year shoot days by jurisdiction (Rec & Parks, Presidio, GG NRA, SFO, …)
    crew_hotel_sagaftra.csv (12 rows) fiscal year × category crew hires, SF hotel nights, local SAG-AFTRA hires
    notable_productions.csv (64 rows) one notable production title, director, starring, network/studio

    Source documents:

    Fiscal year URL
    2025 https://media.api.sf.gov/documents/SF_Film_Annual_Report_FY24-25_Final.pdf
    2024 https://media.api.sf.gov/documents/SF_Film_Annual_Report_FY23-24_2.26.pdf
    2023 https://www.sf.gov/sites/default/files/2024-01/Film%20SF%20Impact%20Report%20FY22_23.pdf
    2022 https://www.sf.gov/sites/default/files/2023-05/Film%20SF%20Annual%20Report%20FY21-22_1.pdf

    Caveats:

    • direct_economic_output_usd is blank for FY21-22; Film SF didn’t headline that metric until FY22-23.
    • 2024’s productions_by_type shoot-day sum is 507 vs. the headline 506 (rounding in the source).
    • california_film_commission and sfo columns in jurisdictions.csv are blank for FY21-22 through FY23-24; they were added in the FY24-25 report.

    hengdian/ — Hengdian World Studios

    hengdian_attractions.csv (7 rows)

    The seven major themed environments / studio sets that make up Hengdian World Studios, each tagged with the role it plays (scenic spot, shooting base, live show, etc.).

    hengdian_productions.csv (30 rows)

    Films and TV series filmed at Hengdian, with director, cast, box office (films only), Wikipedia URL, downloaded poster path, and a heuristic mapping to one or more of the seven attractions.

    • Source: English Wikipedia — Hengdian World Studios and each production’s individual article. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hengdian_World_Studios
    • ⚠️ Caveat on filming_attractions: Wikipedia does not publish a production-to-set mapping for Hengdian. The values in this column are heuristic matches generated by mapping each story’s historical era to the attraction whose architectural style recreates that era (e.g., Qing-dynasty palace dramas → Palace of Ming and Qing Dynasties; 1840s–1949 Shanghai settings → Legend of Bund). The match_basis column records the reasoning for each row. Two modern-set productions (DOA: Dead or Alive, No Return) are intentionally blank because they don’t map cleanly to any historical-era attraction.

    hengdian_visitor_attendance.csv (9 rows)

    Annual visitor count to Hengdian World Studios, in millions, for 2011 – 2019. Source: Wikipedia. Later years are not currently reported on the article.

    attraction_photos.csv + attraction_photos/ (12 photos)

    Wikimedia Commons photos of Hengdian attractions, with OSM ID, attraction name, local file path, original URL, license, and credit. All photos are licensed CC BY-SA 4.0 (or compatible); see the license and credit columns.

    posters/ (29 poster images)

    Poster images for the productions in hengdian_productions.csv, downloaded from each production’s English Wikipedia infobox. Used under fair use for non-commercial educational presentation. Two productions in hengdian_productions.csv are missing posters (Iron Road has no infobox image on Wikipedia; one other was unavailable at scrape time).

    shapefiles/ — HengdianStudio.shp (+ companions)

    A polygon shapefile of the seven Hengdian attractions used to render the theme parks on the Tableau map.

    • Source: OpenStreetMap (Hengdian Studios features, exported via QGIS).
    • Companion files: .dbf (attribute table), .prj (projection), .shx (index), .cpg (encoding), .qgz (QGIS project file used to author the export).

    imdb/ — IMDb Top 250

    imdb_top250_clean.csv (499 rows)

    IMDb Top 250 metadata enriched with up to 11 filming-region tags per title and a worldwide box-office figure pulled from each film’s Wikipedia article.

    Column group Columns
    Identity imdb_id, top250_rank, title, year
    Reception imdb_rating, votes
    Production runtime_minutes, genre, director, cast
    Locations region_1 … region_11 (US state or country names)
    Money box_office_usd, box_office_raw, box_office_source
    • Source — Top 250 list and metadata: https://www.imdb.com/chart/top/
    • Source — box office: linked Wikipedia article for each film (box_office_source column).

    movie_location_corpus/ — filming-location corpus (91 movies)

    A scraped corpus of detailed filming-location pages for 91 of the Top 250 films, used by the 3D Globe Explorer.

    Path Contents
    corpus_index.csv (91 rows) one row per movie — IMDb ID, title, year, source URL, paths to cleaned HTML / JSON, director, locations, image count
    corpus.jsonl one JSON record per movie, mirroring the index
    html/ (91 files) a cleaned-up HTML rendering of each movie’s location page, used inside the explorer’s poster sidebar
    json/ (91 files) the same data in structured form
    assets/ (one folder per IMDb ID) locally cached poster + scene images
    • Source: The Worldwide Guide to Movie Locations — http://www.movie-locations.com/ (specifically the per-movie pages linked from the Top 250 index).

    Reproducibility

    Most of the scraping and cleaning steps that produced these CSVs are in the project’s from-scratch/ and data_source/ directories at the repo root — notably data_source/build_imdb_clean.py, data_source/hengdian/build_hengdian.py, and data_source/hengdian/match_attractions.py. They use requests + beautifulsoup4 and identify themselves with a polite User-Agent. Run dates are recorded in each CSV’s snapshot suffix where applicable.

    Licensing

    • DataSF — Film Locations in San Francisco: public domain (CC0 / DataSF open data terms).
    • Film SF annual reports: public records published by the City of San Francisco.
    • Wikipedia / Wikimedia Commons content: CC BY-SA 3.0 / 4.0 unless noted otherwise. Photos retain individual author credits in hengdian/attraction_photos.csv.
    • IMDb metadata and Movie-Locations.com content: used here under fair use for non-commercial educational presentation; ownership remains with the original publishers.

    For the project narrative, dashboards, and 3D globe that consume these datasets, see the parent repository.
